info.name: Phantix Backend API (Full)
info.description:
Full API surface generated from OpenAPI (252 operations) plus a curated **Staff / Admin** folder.

**Base URL:** `https://staging.phantix.site`

## Staff / admin login
1. Open **00 – Staff / Admin** → **Staff login**.
2. Default variables: `staff_email` / `staff_password` (from `STAFF_BOOTSTRAP_*` in `.env`).
3. `staff_token` is saved automatically — use it for `/api/v1/admin/*` and `POST /api/v1/staff`.
4. **Do not** use organization JWT for admin routes.

## Org dual-control
Mutating org routes include `X-Dual-Control-Session: {{dc_session}}` (set via org-user auth login).

Import: Postman → Import → Upload this file (not raw OpenAPI link).

## Dummy client
Folder **00b – Dummy client happy path** walks register → dual-control → security DB → assets → VAPT plan (see `dummy client setup for phantix/README.md`).

item.name: 00 – Staff / Admin (start here)
item.description:
Platform staff realm is **separate** from organization login.

1. **Staff login** with bootstrap superadmin (created on first empty DB, or via env `STAFF_BOOTSTRAP_EMAIL` / `STAFF_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD`).
2. Token is saved to `staff_token`.
3. Use that token for all `/api/v1/admin/*` and `POST /api/v1/staff` (create more admins).
4. Organization JWT will **not** work on staff/admin routes.

Default bootstrap (from `.env.example`): `admin@example.com` / `change-me-strong-password`.
Also try `admin@phantix.local` if that account exists on your instance.

exec: console.log('Check STAFF_BOOTSTRAP_* or create staff via existing superadmin.');

request.description: Form login. Saves staff_token. Not the org company login.
Requires existing staff JWT with admin/superadmin role.

item.name: 00b – Dummy client happy path
item.description:
End-to-end lab flow for a dummy organization (mirrors `dummy client setup for phantix/README.md`).

Run **top to bottom**. Scripts save `org_token`, `dc_session`, ids.

After dual-control is assigned, mutations send `X-Dual-Control-Session`.
Adjust security DB host/credentials in step 12 for your lab.

request.description: Use a unique slug/email if re-running. Sign-in = primary email.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Dummy Phantix Client",
  "slug": "{{org_slug}}",
  "legal_name": "Dummy Phantix Client Ltd",
  "industry": "technology",
  "company_type": "private_limited",
  "employee_count_range": "11-50",
  "annual_revenue_range": "100k-1m",
  "website": "https://dummy-phantix-client.local",
  "phone": "+1234567890",
  "address_line1": "100 Demo Street",
  "city": "Testville",
  "state_province": "State",
  "postal_code": "12345",
  "country": "US",
  "email": "{{org_email}}",
  "secondary_email": "ops@dummy-phantix-client.local",
  "password": "{{org_password}}",
  "primary_contact": {
    "title": "mr",
    "name": "Test Admin",
    "email": "{{org_email}}",
    "phone": "+1234567890"
  },
  "secondary_contact": {
    "title": "ms",
    "name": "Test Ops",
    "email": "ops@dummy-phantix-client.local",
    "phone": "+1234567891"
  },
  "security_mailbox": "security@dummy-phantix-client.local",
  "compliance_frameworks": [
    "iso_27001"
  ],
  "data_types_handled": [
    "pii",
    "financial"
  ],
  "infrastructure_types": [
    "cloud"
  ],
  "cloud_providers": [
    "aws",
    "gcp"
  ],
  "preferred_services": [
    "vulnerability_management"
  ],
  "security_maturity": "developing",
  "timezone": "America/New_York"
}

request.description: Creates campaign and STARTS it (run_inline). Requires assets in security DB (inventory_snapshot.total > 0). If start fails, response includes campaign_id + next_steps; then POST /vapt/campaigns/{{campaign_id}}/start after adding assets. Lab: set skip_asset_validation=true.
Use when execute left campaign in draft (e.g. assets added later).
Cheap liveness for load balancers / orchestrators. Does not probe dependencies.

item.name: GET Get platform Nmap / discovery settings
PUT Update Nmap default flags, ports, and client policy

request.description:
Configure the real Nmap binary and **admin-specified scanning flags**.

Example body:
```json
{
  "nmap_enabled": true,
  "nmap_binary_path": "nmap",
  "nmap_default_flags": ["-sT", "-sV", "-T4", "--open"],
  "nmap_default_ports": "top-1000",
  "nmap_allow_client_flags": false,
  "nmap_allow_client_ports": true,
  "nmap_max_runtime_seconds": 600
}
```

Non-root hosts should prefer ``-sT`` (TCP connect) over ``-sS`` (SYN).

header.description: Initiator/authorizer session after dual-control is configured

request.description:
Example:
```json
{
  "initiator_user_id": 1,
  "authorizer_user_id": 2,
  "require_dual_control": true
}
```
Users must already exist under this organization and be active.
Initiator and authorizer must be **different** users.

Bootstrap: company JWT until dual-control is first configured.
After that: only initiator/authorizer with authenticator session.

request.description:

item.name: GET Public privacy messaging (what Phantix stores vs customer data)

request.description: Structured copy for the Organization Setup UI. Explains platform account data, dedicated security DB, and what Phantix does **not** store from customer production systems.

Onboard a **company**.

**Company data:** identity, address, industry, security posture, account emails.

**Contact persons** (CISO / IT) — limited PII:
- `title` (mr / mrs / ms / miss / dr / …)
- `name`, `email`, `phone`
- `whatsapp_username`, `telegram_username` — security incident alerts

**Account emails:**
- `email` — primary sign-in
- `secondary_email` — recovery & communications (cannot log in)

After register + login, complete **Organization Setup** (`GET /me/setup`): privacy acceptance, **email OTP**, and optional company modes (domain DNS/HTTP, CAC/RC, manual review).

item.name: GET Catalog of preferred services (for registration / settings UI)

request.description: Catalog from admin-editable experience configs (what preferred_services unlock).

Authenticates Phantix platform staff. Returns a **staff** JWT (`type=staff`) that works on `/api/v1/admin/*` and staff support routes. Organization tokens from `/organizations/login` are **not** accepted there.

item.name: POST Run safe server optimization actions

request.description:
Apply safe in-process optimisations:

| Action | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| `gc_collect` | Full Python GC (gen 0–2) |
| `dispose_db_pool` | Recycle SQLAlchemy connections |
| `clear_idle_tool_locks` | Drop idle per-org scan tool locks |
| `process_pending_alerts` | Drain alert delivery queue |
| `all` | Run every action above |

Does **not** kill workers or purge Redis queues.

item.name: GET Server process + resource overview with health score

request.description:
Full operational picture of this API process and related workers:

- process RSS/CPU/threads
- related uvicorn/celery/alert_daemon PIDs
- host CPU/memory/disk/load
- DB pool, asyncio tasks, GC, tool locks
- Celery workers (if Redis up)
- health score + optimization recommendations
